Eight AI-Built Browser Tools I Maintained Today




I am running a small AI programming outbound experiment around free browser tools. Today I opened the assets, checked indexing basics, submitted URLs, and reinforced the weak sites with public backlinks.




The eight tools:




iWorkViewer: https://iworkviewer.com/

LivePhotoKit: https://livephotokit.com/

PlantingCalendar: https://plantingcalendar.net/

FreeTDEE: https://freetdee.com/

BabyPercent: https://babypercent.com/

InvoicePad: https://invoicepad.net/

ZonePlan: https://zoneplan.net/

PupVax: https://pupvax.com/




Weak-site reinforcement:




FreeTDEE calorie calculator: https://freetdee.com/calorie-calculator/

PupVax puppy vaccine schedule: https://pupvax.com/puppy-vaccine-schedule/

ZonePlan global meeting planner: https://zoneplan.net/global-meeting-planner/

ZonePlan world clock meeting planner: https://zoneplan.net/world-clock-meeting-planner/

InvoicePad invoice templates: https://invoicepad.net/invoice-templates/




Public evidence:




Daily indexing note: https://github.com/Gavin1901/free-browser-tools-index/blob/master/daily/2026–07–16-ai-tool-indexing-maintenance.md




Backlink reinforcement note: https://github.com/Gavin1901/free-browser-tools-index/blob/master/daily/2026–07–16-backlink-reinforcement.md




Full public index: https://github.com/Gavin1901/free-browser-tools-index




This is the boring part of AI programming outbound: not just building tools, but keeping them live, crawlable, linked, and verifiable.
